mechanism_narrative: A sustained high federal funds rate indicates a restrictive monetary policy, which increases the cost of capital for businesses and reduces the present value of future earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are consistently positive (average > 0.5%) during periods when FEDFUNDS is above its 12-month moving average.

mechanism_narrative: A significant month-over-month decline in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates weakening consumer demand and confidence, which directly impacts corporate revenues and earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a short 5-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines by more than 1.5% month-over-month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in more than 60% of instances where RSAFS declines by over 1.5% MoM.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) and a decline in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals a deteriorating labor market, reducing consumer spending power and corporate profitability, which leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months AND PAYEMS declines for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive or flat (average change < 0.1%) during periods where both UNRATE and PAYEMS conditions are met.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent high inflation (CPIAUCSL) combined with a restrictive monetary policy (high FEDFUNDS) creates an environment of elevated discount rates and reduced real earnings, dampening equity valuations and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are consistently positive (average > 0.5%) when both CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS are above their respective thresholds.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in housing starts (HOUST) indicates robust economic activity and strong consumer confidence, which typically translates into broader economic growth and positive corporate earnings, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are consistently negative (average < -0.2%) during periods of sustained HOUST growth (e.g., 3 consecutive months of increase).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in industrial production (INDPRO) signals a significant slowdown in manufacturing and overall economic output, which directly impacts corporate revenues and investor sentiment, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines by more than 1.0% month-over-month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 50% of triggered instances despite the INDPRO decline.

mechanism_narrative: A low and stable unemployment rate (UNRATE) signifies a healthy and tight labor market, supporting consumer spending and corporate profitability, which generally leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are consistently negative (average < -0.1%) when UNRATE is below 4.5% for 3 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A significant deceleration in real GDP growth (GDPC1) combined with a decline in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates a broad-based weakening of aggregate demand, which severely impacts corporate earnings and investor confidence, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annual growth rate falls below 1% AND RSAFS declines for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are consistently positive (average > 0.5%) during periods where both GDP growth is low and retail sales are declining.
